title: pr-minder config
description: Configuration for the pr-minder GitHub App. Supports JSONC (comments allowed). Place at .github/config/pr-minder/pr-minder.jsonc in any repo, or at the same path in the org's .github repo (with optional per-repo overrides under 'repos').

$schema.description: URL to this JSON Schema. Used by IDEs for validation and autocomplete.

repos.description: Per-repo config overrides. Only meaningful in the org-level .github/config/pr-minder/pr-minder.jsonc; ignored in per-repo config files.

triggerCondition.description: A single trigger condition. All specified keys must match (AND). Multiple conditions in the triggers array are ORed.

approved_by.description: PR must be approved by at least one of these GitHub usernames.

min_approvals.description: PR must have at least this many approvals from any users.

autoUpdatePr.description: Approval-gate settings for branch updates. Any passing condition fires update-branch.

triggers.description: List of trigger conditions (ORed). Keys within a condition are ANDed.

autoTriggerWorkflows.description: Re-trigger CI for 'zombie' PRs that have no workflow runs of their own. A PR created with the default GITHUB_TOKEN (author github-actions[bot]) never triggers its workflows (GitHub suppresses recursive runs); on the `opened` event pr-minder revives those. On the `reopened` event it revives any open PR that still has zero workflow runs, whatever its author. The fix is a close+reopen with the App's own credentials, which fires a fresh `pull_request.reopened` event that runs the workflows. Opt-in; defaults to false.

autoOpenPr.description: Automatically open a pull request for any branch that is ahead of its base and has no open PR. PRs are opened with pr-minder's own App credentials, so (unlike PRs created by a workflow's default GITHUB_TOKEN) they trigger their workflows normally and are never 'zombie' PRs. Fires on a push to a non-default branch, and sweeps existing branches when the App is installed or repositories are added. The default branch and gh-pages are always skipped. By default the base is detected from each branch's fork point (base_from_fork_point, on by default) — the branch it was created from — falling back to target_base (or the default branch); set base_from_fork_point to false to always open into target_base instead.

enabled.description: Turn the feature on. Opt-in; defaults to false.

skip_branches.description: Additional branch names (exact match) to never open PRs for. The default branch and gh-pages are always skipped regardless.

skip_branch_patterns.description: Additional branches to never open PRs for, as regular-expression patterns matched against the branch name (anchor with ^...$ for a full-name match). Use this to exclude a whole family of branches at once — e.g. ["^\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+$"] excludes version branches like 2.1.81, so they never each become a PR head. A malformed pattern simply never matches.

target_base.description: Base branch for opened PRs. Defaults to the repository's default branch. When base_from_fork_point is enabled this is the fallback base used when no fork point is detected.

base_from_fork_point.description: Detect each branch's base from its fork point instead of always using target_base: pr-minder walks the branch's commits newest-first and uses the first ancestor that is the HEAD of another qualifying branch (the default branch, or one matching base_branch_patterns). In an archive repo a working branch forked from a long-lived branch (e.g. a version branch) is opened back into that branch; a branch forked off the default branch goes into the default branch. Falls back to target_base when no qualifying fork point is found. On by default; set to false to always open into target_base (or the default branch).

base_branch_patterns.description: Optional restriction on which branches fork-point detection may select as a base, as regular-expression patterns matched against the branch name. Empty (the default) means ANY branch can be a base, so a branch is routed to whatever branch it was forked from. When non-empty, only the repository's default branch or a branch matching one of these patterns may be a base (a non-matching fork parent is ignored, falling back to target_base). E.g. ["^\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+$"] restricts auto-detected bases to version branches for an archive layout.

close_when_empty.description: Close any open non-draft PR whose net diff against its base is zero (no changed files), regardless of who opened it — a 0-diff PR has nothing to review or merge and only wastes CI on each update. It doesn't matter how the PR became empty (a squash-merge orphan that's ahead by commit count but has no net content, a PR whose change landed in the base another way, etc.) or who authored it; a human's empty PR is closed too (closing is reversible — it can be reopened). Only an EXACTLY-zero changed-files count closes a PR; an unknown count (GitHub omitted the file list) is left alone. Runs when the default branch advances (a push to it) and on the install/backfill sweeps. Defaults to true.

delete_branch_when_empty.description: When closing a content-empty PR (see close_when_empty), also delete its head branch, so closed branches don't pile up. The branch is content-identical to its base (zero net diff), so deleting it loses nothing. Only branches in this repository are deleted — a fork PR's head branch (which lives in the fork) is left untouched — and GitHub itself refuses to delete the default or a protected branch. Has no effect unless close_when_empty is also on. Off by default: deleting a branch is the one part of the empty-PR cleanup that isn't trivially reversible.

autoDescribePr.description: Generate the PR title and description from the PR's full diff with an AI model, whenever a PR is opened (or marked ready for review) and on every new commit. The description is rewritten each time the diff changes (the existing description is passed to the model so still-accurate notes are preserved); the title is replaced only when the existing one is a missing/autogenerated placeholder. The model endpoint and credentials are configured on the Worker, not here.

enabled.description: Turn the feature on. Opt-in; defaults to false.

model.description: Override the default model name for this repo/org (the default is configured on the pr-describe webhook).

labelOptions.description: Settings for a single label name.

auto_add.description: If 'on_pr_creation', automatically apply this label to PRs when they are opened. 'false' or omitted disables auto-add.

create_label_if_missing_in_repo.description: If true, create this label in the repo (with the configured color) if it does not already exist.

color.description: Hex color used when creating this label, with or without a leading '#'.

mode.description: 'auto_update': PR branch is kept in sync with its base whenever this label is present (complementary to auto_update_pr.triggers). 'auto_merge': adding/removing this label enables/disables GitHub's native auto-merge; toggling auto-merge via the GitHub UI adds/removes the label. 'merge_conflict': pr-minder applies this label to a PR when it has a merge conflict with its base, and removes it once the conflict is resolved (managed entirely by pr-minder; do not add or remove it by hand).

auto_merge_method.description: Merge method to use when enabling auto-merge. Only relevant when mode is 'auto_merge'.

autoLabelPr.description: Map of label name to its settings.

repoConfig.description: Per-repo overrides. Supports the same fields as the top-level config, except 'repos'.
